meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

items.label: 1a
items.text: If A and B are m×n, then both AB^T and A^T B are defined.

items.label: 1b
items.text: If AB = C and C has 2 columns, then A has 2 columns.

items.label: 1c
items.text: Left-multiplying a matrix B by a diagonal matrix A, with nonzero entries on the diagonal, scales the rows of B.

items.label: 1d
items.text: If BC = BD, then C = D.

items.label: 1e
items.text: If AC = 0, then either A = 0 or C = 0.

items.label: 1f
items.text: If A and B are n×n, then (A + B)(A − B) = A^2 − B^2.

items.label: 1g
items.text: An elementary n×n matrix has either n or n+1 nonzero entries.

items.label: 1h
items.text: The transpose of an elementary matrix is an elementary matrix.

items.label: 1i
items.text: An elementary matrix must be square.

items.label: 1j
items.text: Every square matrix is a product of elementary matrices.

items.label: 1k
items.text: If A is a 3×3 matrix with three pivot positions, there exist elementary matrices E1,…,Ep such that Ep⋯E1A = I.

items.label: 1l
items.text: If AB = I, then A is invertible.

items.label: 1m
items.text: If A and B are square and invertible, then AB is invertible, and (AB)^-1 = A^-1 B^-1.

items.label: 1n
items.text: If AB = BA and if A is invertible, then A^-1 B = BA^-1.

items.label: 1o
items.text: If A is invertible and if r ≠ 0, then (rA)^-1 = rA^-1.

items.label: 1p
items.text:
If A is a 3×3 matrix and the equation Ax = [
1
0
0
] has a unique solution, then A is invertible.
